title: WOW2 Filter Overview: Part 1 - Basic Parameters
description:
WOW2 Filter Review: http://edmprod.com/sugar-bytes-wow2-review/

Sign up to our newsletter: http://eepurl.com/BcVrf

Get 50% off our Introduction to Electronic Music Production Course: https://www.udemy.com/learn-electronic-music-production/?couponCode=EDMPROD

Want to learn more? http://www.edmprod.com/about

Looking to contribute an article or video tutorial? http://www.edmprod.com/contribute
transcript: hey everyone uh so the other day i posted a review of this plugin the wow 2 filter box on edmpro.com quite a long review i'd recommend go ahead and read if you're interested in buying this plugin but today i'm just going to give a bit of an overview of the some of the features in this plugin so we're just going to be looking at these five knobs here and this filter section and in the in the next parts we'll look at the vowel mode and also so this modulation section uh below the first thing is you'll notice when you open up this plug-in is the cut-off knob as you would expect on the filter now i really like this cutoff knob um first of all it looks great i mean it's this plugin is really well designed but i like this thing in the middle um and it basically shows you a visual representation of what the filter is doing to your sound it's minimal it's simple and i like it you can imagine the frequency range inside here and the filter cutting this off which is real cool and of course it changes with whatever filter you're using for example if i change to a high pass um you can see that it switches around or maybe a cone filter does that you get the idea so that's real cool and of course it works if i play a sound here you can see the effect it has cut off knob real awesome um often i find it quicker to just look in the middle here instead of finding out where this little thing is but you know each of their own next thing is resonance and i really like your resonance on this because it doesn't it's hard to make it sound harsh um i forgot how they sort they do this they handle it in such a way but when you're using cheap filters especially a 3d stock filter and you push the resonance too high you can often get self oscillation which sounds really harsh and it's not nice to have at all but on this filter it's quite hard to push it to that extent i can show you what i mean so like i said it's hard to push it to that that pain point where it really hurts your ears and just doesn't sound nice at all so that's your resonance and you can also see that it changes um the little diagram in there changes and it's also great to use with vowel mode which we'll get into in the next part next thing is volume and this is just a typical output control so if you need to give it a bit more boost or impact then you can turn the volume up or down if say the distortion is making it too loud then you've got your dry wet which is a pretty standard feature on all filters and it's called have if you want subtle filter effects now finally down here we've got distortion and i really like distortion uh the distortion on this filter because it's just so okay a is good sounding and b you've got seven different um distortion types and you can also put it after or before the filter which i really like so if you want say low pass distortion then you'll put it before the filter like so but if i put it after the filter it distorts um you know obviously distorts the wave the audio signal after the filter has been added this might be a bit loud but i'll show you the difference so that's a real cool um feature and of course you've got different distortion types it's one of my favorite um bit crusher all that kind of stuff real cool and i think that's one of the things that really sets this filter apart from the others because there are other filters out there which do offer a lot of features including modulation but i think the wow 2 really exceeds in that aspect and also the the filter types they sound really nice and there's a few modeled off the moog um i really like the default one which is the o3o and you've got a few high pass band pass and special ones which are really cool too overall and more than enough filters i think i only use a couple of them it's just really easy to understand easy to use plugin so in the next part i'm going to go over the vowel mode and then we'll look at some of these things down here so hope you enjoyed the video and i'll see you in the next one
